case_title: CALVIN MCRAY VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL

REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF ITS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 59709841) FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRE UNDERWRITERS ASSOCIATION FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-17-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE UNDERWRITERS ASSOCIATION, AND FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP'S DEMURRER TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF CALVIN MCRAY HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS. MR. MCRAY ADEQUATELY PLEADS BAD FAITH LIABILITY BASED ON AN ALTER EGO THEORY. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT THAT MR. MCRAY ATTACH THE INSURANCE POLICY TO THE COMPLAINT. MR. MCRAY HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THE LEGAL EFFECT OF THE INSURANCE POLICY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY ANN DEYOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Continued To Feb-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2016 Set For Trial On Aug-07-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Fire Insurance Exchange, Fire Underwriters Association, and Farmers Insurance Group's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. Plaintiff Calvin McRay has adequately alleged claims for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing against all defendants. Even if the court took judicial notice of the printouts from the U.S Patent and Trademark Office website, California Department of Insurance website, and California Secretary of State website, those printouts do not conclusively negate Mr. McRay's allegations against Fire Underwriters Association and Farmers Insurance Group. Mr. McRay adequately pleads bad faith liability based on an alter ego theory. There is no requirement that Mr. McRay attach the insurance policy to the complaint. Mr. McRay has sufficiently pled the legal effect of the insurance policy. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
